items.question: I wish I ___ more free time (but I don't).
explanations: 'Have' is present simple; a present wish needs the past simple.
Correct! A present wish takes the past simple: 'I wish I had more free time'.
'Would have' isn't used for your own present state.
'Will have' is a real future, not a wish about now.

items.question: I wish I ___ harder for the exam last month.
explanations: 'Studied' (past simple) is for present wishes; a past regret needs the past perfect.
Correct! A past regret takes the past perfect: 'I wish I had studied harder'.
'Study' is present simple, but this is a past regret.
'Would study' isn't used for your own past actions.

items.question: I wish it ___ raining — I want to go out!
explanations: 'Stopped' describes a present state; here you want a change to happen.
Correct! For a change you want, use 'would': 'I wish it would stop raining'.
'Had stopped' is a past regret, not a present wish for change.
'Stops' is present simple, which doesn't fit a wish.

items.question: I wish I ___ taller.
explanations: 'Am' is present simple, but a wish needs the past form.
This doesn't fit a wish about a present state.
Correct! Use 'were' for all subjects: 'I wish I were taller'.
'Would be' isn't used for your own present state.

items.question: If only she ___ me the truth yesterday!
explanations: 'Told' (past simple) is for present wishes; 'yesterday' needs the past perfect.
Correct! A past regret takes the past perfect: 'If only she had told me'.
'Tells' is present simple, but this is a past regret.
'Would tell' would suit a future/habit wish, not a finished past.

items.question: I wish you ___ interrupting me all the time.
explanations: 'Stopped' describes a state; here you want someone to change a habit.
Correct! For an annoying habit you want changed, use 'would': 'I wish you would stop'.
'Had stopped' is a past regret, not a request to change.
'Stop' is a bare imperative, not the wish structure.

items.question: Correct this: 'I wish I have a car.'
options: I wish I had a car.
I wish I have had a car.
I wish I would have a car.
explanations: Correct! A present wish takes the past simple: 'I wish I had a car'.
'Have had' is present perfect, which doesn't fit a wish.
'Would have' isn't used for your own present state.
'I wish I have' uses the present tense, which is wrong after 'I wish'.

items.question: I wish I ___ that job when I had the chance.
explanations: 'Took' (past simple) is for present wishes; a past regret needs the past perfect.
Correct! A past regret takes the past perfect: 'I wish I had taken that job'.
'Take' is present simple, but this is a past regret.
'Would take' isn't used for your own past actions.

items.question: Which one is a PAST regret?
options: I wish I had listened to my parents.
I wish it would warm up.
I wish I were more confident.
explanations: This is a present wish (past simple).
Correct! 'I wish I had listened' (past perfect) is a regret about the past.
This is a wish for a future change (would).
This is a present wish (were).

items.question: Rewrite as a wish: 'I don't speak Spanish.' →
options: I wish I had spoken Spanish.
I wish I would speak Spanish.
explanations: Correct! A present wish takes the past simple: 'I wish I spoke Spanish'.
'I wish I speak' uses the present tense, which is wrong.
'Had spoken' is a past regret, not a present wish.
'Would speak' isn't used for your own present ability.
